title: First Week — Work & School
description: Anna and Peter help you settle into your new job and enrol your child at the local school.
context: It is your first week in Slovakia. Anna is your neighbour and works nearby. She and her husband Peter have kids at the local school and offer to help you settle in — both at work and with enrolling your child.

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.text: Dobrý deň! Vitajte v práci. Som Anna, vaša kolegyňa.
exchanges.translation: Good day! Welcome to work. I am Anna, your colleague.
exchanges.prompt: Greet Sara and say you are pleased to be here.

choices.translation: Good day. I am pleased.
Good day. Where is the school?
Good day. I am hungry.
Good day. I want to leave.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Teším sa is a natural response when meeting a new colleague.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Teším sa to say you are pleased to meet someone.

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.text: Pracujeme od ôsmej do štvrtej. Je to pre vás v poriadku?
exchanges.translation: We work from eight to four. Is that okay for you?

choices.translation: Yes, that is fine.
No, I want to work at night.
I do not know where to work.
I want a break.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Je to v poriadku confirms the schedule is acceptable.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Je to v poriadku to say something is fine.

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.prompt: Say you worked in your home country.

choices.translation: I worked in my home country.
I worked here my whole life.
I never worked.
I work at school.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Pracoval som vo svojej krajine explains your previous work location.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Pracoval som to talk about where you worked before.

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.translation: Do you have children? Do they have school?
exchanges.prompt: Say yes and that you need to enrol your child at school.

choices.text: Áno, potrebujem zapísať dieťa do školy.
choices.translation: Yes, I need to enrol my child at school.

choices.text: Áno, moje dieťa je v práci.
choices.translation: Yes, my child is at work.
No, I have no children.
Yes, the children have a car.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Potrebujem zapísať dieťa do školy is the key phrase for school enrolment.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Potrebujem zapísať dieťa do školy when you need to enrol your child.

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.text: Škola je blízko. Musíte ísť k riaditeľke. Máte rodný list dieťaťa?
exchanges.translation: The school is nearby. You must go to the principal. Do you have your child's birth certificate?
exchanges.prompt: Say you have the birth certificate.

choices.translation: Yes, I have the birth certificate.
No, I have no documents.
I only have a passport.
What is a birth certificate?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám rodný list confirms you have the required document.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Mám rodný list to confirm you have the birth certificate.

exchanges.speaker: riaditelka
exchanges.text: Dobrý deň. Chcete zapísať dieťa k nám?
exchanges.translation: Good day. Do you want to enrol your child here?
exchanges.prompt: Confirm yes, you want to enrol your child here.

choices.translation: Yes, I want to enrol my child.
No, I am going home.
How much does school cost?
Where is the canteen?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, chcem zapísať dieťa is the direct confirmation the principal needs.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Chcem zapísať dieťa to express your intention to enrol.

exchanges.speaker: riaditelka
exchanges.prompt: Tell the principal your child is 7 years old.

choices.translation: He/She is seven years old.
He/She is thirty years old.
I do not know how old he/she is.
He/She is ten months old.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Má sedem rokov gives the child's age correctly.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Má sedem rokov to say the child is seven years old.

exchanges.speaker: riaditelka
exchanges.prompt: Say not yet but the child is learning.

choices.translation: Not yet, but he/she is learning.
Yes, perfectly.
The child does not go to school.
He/She only speaks English.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Zatiaľ nie, ale učí sa is honest and positive — a perfect answer.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Zatiaľ nie, ale učí sa to say not yet but learning.

exchanges.speaker: riaditelka
exchanges.text: Škola začína o ôsmej. Obedná prestávka je od dvanástej do jednej.
exchanges.translation: School starts at eight. Lunch break is from twelve to one.
exchanges.prompt: Say you understand and ask which day school finishes early.

choices.translation: I understand. Which day does it finish early?
I do not understand. Where is the canteen?
Good. The child will not eat.
At eight? That is late.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Rozumiem plus a follow-up question shows you understood and want to know more.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Rozumiem to confirm you understood, then ask Ktorý deň končí skôr?

exchanges.speaker: anna
exchanges.text: Výborne! Dieťa začína v pondelok. Peter a ja sme radi, že vám pomohli. Ako sa cítite po prvom týždni?
exchanges.translation: Excellent! The child starts on Monday. Peter and I are glad we could help. How do you feel after the first week?
exchanges.prompt: Say you feel good and thank Anna for her help.

choices.text: Cítim sa dobre. Ďakujem za pomoc.
choices.translation: I feel good. Thank you for the help.
I feel bad. I want to go home.
I do not know how I feel.
I am tired. Where is the bus?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Cítim sa dobre. Ďakujem za pomoc is a warm and natural close.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Cítim sa dobre to say you feel good and Ďakujem za pomoc to thank her.

completionMessage: Excellent! You navigated your first week in Slovakia with Anna and Peter's help — confirmed your work schedule, enrolled your child at school, and spoke with the principal. You are settling in well!
